item.title: Feeling Don’t Care About Your Facts (Ep. 332)
item.dc:creator: Ari Bennett
item.description:
…
Feeling Don’t Care About Your Facts (Ep. 332)
Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky
Download

Sponsor (Yehoshua Sova):

I just went through ALL 326 Shiurim at double speed the past few months- GADZOOKS! Been a follower of yours since the Platinum Platonic Relationships tape. Also had to stop running/driving like Rabbis Sharfman/Krohn to prevent any collateral damage deaths due to laughter from the show. I would love to hear the Rav's take on the following: Red Flags I have seen in my life and how YOU can learn from my mistakes. For example:
- The unfortunate situations of the ex son in laws- surely there were things that you saw and how we could be protected (besides the people who withheld info). Your Daughters should find their Bashert soon and enough of the pain that they are experiencing!
- Psychotic teachers/adminstrators.
- Dealing with "real" crazies- not the Rabbi Orlofsky ones!
- What was so dark about being a Mashgiach?
- Who was someone that you looked up to as a role model and had it dashed and the signs were there for you to see the entire time?
The truth is I would love to host you on my TorahAnytime channel and 24/6 channel (I am also not on Naki Radio ;-)) as a podcast interview and this sponsorship ploy I am doing now was actually suggested by you! This opener of questions I always had can be part of a continued list of pressing questions I would love to ask at a later time if the Rav would like to entertain such an idea. Wishing continued Hatzlacha with all of the VERY powerful Divrei Chizuk mixed with great pop culture references and songs! Your Talmid- Rabbi Yehoshua Sova - Miami Beach

📝 Submit your question: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

🟢 Join the “Rabbi Orlofsky Show” WhatsApp group: https://chat.whatsapp.com/IOEyfUQ0A8WC7yNqWsWsWS

~~~

Follow Rabbi Orlofsky around the web!

🎙 Live online shiurim: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/live

📖 Meaningful Tefila series: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

📅 Event schedule: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/events

✉️ Send feedback: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/contact

🤗 Sponsorships: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

📣 Live speaking engagements: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/book-events

Other platforms:

• https://torahanytime.com/#/speaker?l=256

• https://facebook.com/rabbiorlofsky

• https://twitter.com/RabbiOrlofsky

• https://youtube.com/rabbiorlofsky

~~~

"The Rabbi Orlofsky Show Theme”

Composed and Performed by Lenny Solomon

Learn more at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/music

item.title: Love To Disagree (Ep. 329)
item.dc:creator: Ari Bennett
item.description:
Sponsored By: YOUR LOCAL LEVY

During Sefirah, when we focus on bein adam l’chaveiro, how should we relate to different sectors within Judaism—not just within Orthodoxy, but more broadly? If I believe that certain groups are making serious mistakes or are fundamentally wrong in their approach, what is my obligation? On the one hand, I understand that I should view all Jews as part of Hashem’s people and relate to them with a sense of unity. On the other hand, is it acceptable to distance myself and “love from afar” while still disagreeing with them? Within Orthodoxy, even with differences between groups, there is generally a shared framework of avodat Hashem. My question is mainly about relating across more distinct sectors, such as Dati Leumi/Mizrachi, Charedi, and Reform. Do I have an obligation to genuinely question my own assumptions and consider that they may be serving Hashem more correctly than I am, or can I remain committed to my own mesorah while still maintaining respect and a sense of unity? thank you kvod harav!

item.description: Suri and Chaim Boiangiu are delighted to dedicate a podcast in honor of our children Rabbi Ari and Grace Boiangiu, Rabbi Sholom Yaakov and Chanie Boiangiu and Yigal and Erica Sasportas in recognition of their midos tovos and their love for am yisroel. May they continue to bring up their children and grandchildren with the same midos.Rabbi Orlofsky, you have been a role model and inspiration for all the Boiangius and Sasportases for over 20 years. May you continue מחיל אל חיל.

item.title: The Joy Of Pesach Prep (Ep. 322)
item.dc:creator: Ari Bennett
item.description:
Sponsored By: Yehuda Winzelberg

Sefer Orchos Chaim L'HaRosh is a timeless Mussar classic, enhanced with hundreds of stories and insights. The Sefer Orchos Chaim L’HaRosh has been treasured as a guide to living with yiras Shamayim, refined middos, and true Torah values. Written by Rabbeinu Asher ben Yechiel, the Rosh, who was one of the great Rishonim. This work has been learned in the great yeshivos of the past and present, and it is used as a roadmap for avodas Hashem and personal growth. This new edition, by Rabbi Yehuda Winzelberg, has opened up this work and geared it to our generation. It is filled with uplifting insights, practical guidance, and great stories, that bring the Rosh’s teachings into sharp focus for our lives today. The Sefer Orchos Chaim L’haRosh will elevate the way you think, speak, and live. Available at Seforim stores and at the Judaica Press website. https://judaicapress.com/collections/new-releases/products/orchos-chaim-lharosh

item.title: Purim: When Hashem Hides (Ep. 318)
item.dc:creator: Ari Bennett
item.description:
Sponsored by Anonymous:

Dear Rabbi Orlofsky, It has been 30 years since I had the zchus of having you as my Mashgiach Ruchani at Yeshiva Ohr David. Looking back, it is clear that your guidance didn't just impact my years in Yeshiva; it has shaped the trajectory of my life and, by extension, the lives of my children. It brings me immense pleasure to see the genuine excitement my two daughters have whenever a new "Rabbi Orlofsky Show" is released. Your Torah and your perspective have become a staple in our home. In fact, one of my daughters is currently in seminary, and after a few months in the "system," she has officially concluded that the world is missing one vital institution: A Rabbi Orlofsky Seminary for Girls Who Want to Hear the Truth and Get the Most Out of their Year. I would like to dedicate this shiur in honor of my Aishes Chayil, Ana and my children, Danielle and Aliza. They make me so proud every single day with their tireless dedication to chesed, kiruv, and avodas Hashem. Finally, please consider this dedication a completely transparent and lightly veiled bribe. My daughter is ready and willing to work at your Pesach program - she’s hardworking, high-energy, and already knows all your jokes, so she’s basically overqualified. If that doesn't work, we’ll settle for a Shabbos invite. We promise to laugh at all the stories we’ve already heard on the show.

Pesach Program:

https://www.rabbiorlofsky.com/pesach-5786

Phone / WhatsApp - Tzippy at Castles: 02-538-9911

Email - castles@castlesil.com

item.description:
Sponsored by Shimon Bernholtz:

לעילוי נשמת ר׳ שלום חיים בן נחום Dear Rabbi Orlofsky, Thank you for the tremendous chizuk your shiurim give to me and my family. They say, “Those who understand, teach.” And you teach in a way that helps us understand not just the Torah, but how to live it with simcha. The way you blend Torah, avodas hamiddos, and stories of Gedolim, along with that contagious laughter, is pure gold. And yes... I even use your jokes, beshem omro, though the delivery is definitely better on your side of the microphone. Wishing you continued hatzlacha, much nachas, and many more years of spreading Torah, chizuk, and joy around the world. Shkoyach!.
item.itunes:author: Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky

item.title: Listeners' questions (Ep. 296)
item.dc:creator: Ari Bennett
item.description:
Sponsored by Yosef and Dr. Chana Richman

Hi Rabbi Orlofsky, my wife and I love your show and we gain so much from listening to you. We love your powerful insights and incredible sense of humor and we are constantly amazed by how you use both to weave your way through so many complicated topics, all the while coming out with a practical real world application. I wanted to sponsor your show in honor of my wife on our 5th anniversary on September 6th, or the 17 of Elul. I wanted to thank Hashem for blessing me with such an aishes chayil, and my prayer is that all the singles in klal Israel who are looking to get married will be blessed with finding such an aishes chayil, if not before this Rosh Hashanah certainly married before the next. Lastly, I wanted to give you (Rav Orlofsky) a bracha that you should continue to share Torah in good health and strength for many years to come.
item.itunes:author: Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky

item.title: Hashem will comfort us (Ep. 292)
item.dc:creator: Ari Bennett
item.description: Hi K'vod Harav, I'm the same sponsor from this week's episode, I just wanted to clarify that I don't deserve as much credit as it may have come off as, I had discovered the Rav's shiurim and podcasts about six years ago, when the podcast was still in its first few weeks (I think maybe episode 6, or somewhere around there), so there weren't that many podcasts yet at the time to catch up on. When I discovered it though I did go back to find and listen to all of the Rav's previous shiurim that I could find online. Thank you again K'vod Harav for everything the Rav does and for all of the chizzuk and inspiration that we all gain from the Rav's shiurim and podcasts. May the Rav always continue to inspire us, and may we all grow in Torah and all good things, and be zocheh to see mashiach and the binyan beit hamikdash bimheira beyameinu amen.
item.itunes:author: Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky

item.title: From Nine to Ten (Ep. 290)
item.dc:creator: Ari Bennett
item.description:
Sponsored by Anonymous

Dear Rabbi Orlofsky, Thank you K'vod Harav for everything the Rav does, and for all his shiurim and podcasts. I am one of those who, when I discovered the Rav's shiurim/podcasts, went back and watched any and all of the Rav's shiurim that I could find online, going back as far as possible.

I would like to sponsor the episode le'iluy nishmat Rachamim ben Geula, whose yahrtzeit is on tisha b’av

Thank you again K'vod Harav. May the Rav continue to be marbitz torah barabim and give chizzuk and inspiration to many more people 'ad 120 and beyond, with health, happiness, and all good things, and may we be zocheh to see mashiach bimheira beyameinu amen.

item.description: Sponsored by Canada Rosens (again) Thank you for your fresh and very practical perspective on Torah and Yiddishkeit. In particular, your re-framing of the Aguna "Crisis" into a choice between marriage v.s. pilegesh was excellent. It's a great example of how the world uses inflammatory positioning to get us to lose sight of the ikkur issue. Keep up the great work.

item.title: Nobody likes us (Ep. 286)
item.dc:creator: Ari Bennett
item.description: Sponsored by Annoymous. I'm finally sponsoring an episode! I decided that it's about time to show at least an itsy-bitsy physical amount of hakaras hatov to Rabbi Orlofsky and his show. I'm not exaggerating or being dramatic (though I've been known for such things) when I say that it has saved my life. It reminds me of the little and big joys in life and in Judaism, and I am so grateful. The Rabbi Orlofsky show makes me feel good and reminds me why I want to continue on my life's journey. I love your laugh and the sense of community it gives me.
item.itunes:author: Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky
item.itunes:summary: Sponsored by Annoymous. I'm finally sponsoring an episode! I decided that it's about time to show at least an itsy-bitsy physical amount of hakaras hatov to Rabbi Orlofsky and his show. I'm not exaggerating or being dramatic (though I've been known for such things) when I say that it has saved my life. It reminds me of the little and big joys in life and in Judaism, and I am so grateful. The Rabbi Orlofsky show makes me feel good and reminds me why I want to continue on my life's journey. I love your laugh and the sense of community it gives me.

item.title: Dreidels Are NOT Actually Made Out Of Clay (Ep. 264)
item.dc:creator: Yisroel Yudkowsky
item.description:
Sponsored by The Satisfied Self.

Do you ever wish you could enjoy food more without feeling controlled by it?

Are you looking for a way to stop emotional eating without guilt or shame?

The Satisfied Self can help!

Shoshana Schwartz is an addiction and codependency specialist who’s helped over 150 women break free from emotional eating and other self-sabotaging habits.

The course starts January 6 and spaces are limited. 

You can check out the details online - just look up thesatisfiedself.com

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

item.title: Rosh Hashanah - Recipes And Inspiration (Ep. 254)
item.dc:creator: Yisroel Yudkowsky
item.description:
Sponserd by Shawn Karp, CPA.

L’ilui Nishmas:

Tzvi Meir Ben Reb Yehuda - Harold Schechter (Yahrtzeit 24 Elul).

Yissochur Dov Ben Mordechai Yosef HaKohain - Barry Hochdorf (Yahrtzeit 23 Elul).

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

~~~

The q&a is sponsored by Teenspace. 

Teenspace is a new hotline for teenage girls (and their mothers:) which provides Kosher and fun entertainment and inspiration.

We have top of the line content- like Mrs. Yehudis Kormornick, author of The Last Slave, Mrs. Dena Akerman, published illustrator, Mrs Miriam Swerdlov, Mrs. Chana Appel, medical clown, and more.

Call today in the US at 518-988-1364.

And in Israel at 074-745-0817.

For information and sponsorship opportunities, contact us at teenspaceline@gmail.com.

📝 Submit your question: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

You’re a thoughtful Jewish woman with a thirsty soul. 

It’s been 11 months of war. 11 months of searching for answers and connection.

You know the Torah holds the most satisfying, deep, relevant answers. What would happen if you could actually access them? 

You could change the world! 

You're INVITED! Join the FREE sweet, deep learnfest for growth-oriented women: 

The Rise Into Your Crown Summit V

Join FREE: https://thenexus.org/education-summit/

Access R' Orlofsky's session HERE: https://school.thenexus.org/courses/take/rise-into-your-crown-world-summit-education-2024-free-pass/lessons/58324337-the-essence-of-chinuch-it-s-good-to-be-a-jew-rabbi-dovid-orlofsky

Because when a woman is filled with profound, nourishing, healing Torah, and feels a deep connection to Hashem, it overflows naturally onto the world around her…

Featuring 40 leading voices in Jewish education, hosted by Rebbetzin Tamar Taback, including: Rabbis Ben Zion Shafier, Menachem Nissel, Rebbetzins Tzipora Gottlieb, Shira Smiles, Slovie Wolff, Debbie Selengut, Mrs Faigie Zelcer, Sivan Rahav-Meir, Dina Friedman, Lori Palatnik, and many, many more! 

phoneline available. Contact: info@thenexus.org

~~~

📝 Submit your question: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

item.title: SERIES FINALE: Leaving Shabbos or Living Shabbos (Ep. 220)
item.dc:creator: Yisroel Yudkowsky
item.description:
Due to the situation in Eretz Yisrael, the Gedolim have advised to work on our Shmiras Shabbos.

Rabbi Orlofsky has decided to make the next few episodes about this important topic, in order to help us learn more and be Mischazek in our Shmiras Shabbos.

~~~

Sponsored anonymously In honor of the greatest magic shiur!

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

~~~

item.title: Yom Kippur: It’s All About Love (Ep. 202)
item.dc:creator: Yisroel Yudkowsky
item.description:
Twice a year the maidens used to dress in white and dance in the vineyards to attract husbands - on the 15th of Av and on Yom Kippur. 

What’s the connection between starting a relationship and Yom Kippur? Find out in this episode of the Rabbi Orlofsky Show.

item.title: Slichos: Be The Korban (Ep. 200)
item.dc:creator: Yisroel Yudkowsky
item.description:
Why do Ashkenazim Start saying Slichos on Motzai Shabbos?

Why do we have to say Slichos for a minimum of 4 days?

Rabbi Orlofsky dives deep into the topic of Slichos and answers these questions to give us the tools to start our year on the right foot and give us the means to live a better life.

~~~

This episode is sponsored by Tsipora and Shawn Karp L’illui Nishmas Yissachar Dov ben Mordechai Yosef HaKohen and Tzvi Meir ben R' Yehuda

item.title: Chanuka: Fighting for Clarity (Ep. 189)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Chanuka teaches us how to take on apikorsim on their own intellectual level.

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a Zechus for Rabbi Orlofsky and his entire Mishpacha. Thank you so much for all you do for Klal Yisroel!

👉 Sponsor yours!  https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

Besides Lowen's cream puffs, do you have a favorite pastry? (Via anonymous)

What is the Torah's perspective on genetic testing? I someone who got married before this was easily available, and now has medical problems in his family, how could you tell him you weren't supposed to marry this person after building a beautiful family? (Via anonymous)

What are your thoughts about the current nuclear threats coming from Russia? Do you think we are going to experience nuclear destruction? (Via anonymous)

Why do we sell Aliyos in shul? Is it considered good Middos to get a Kibbud if it’s showing off that one can put up more money than someone else? (Via anonymous)

What is the definition of hatred in: לא תשנא את אחיך בלבבך If I dislike someone, when does one start to have an aveira under this לאו? (Via anonymous)

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

Chapters

[1:13] Intro

[2:15] Feedback email

[4:04] Sponsorship https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

[28:58] Q&A: Genetic testing?

[33:58] Q&A: Nuclear threats?

[36:20] Q&A: Why sell aliyos?

[37:20] Q&A: Forbidden to hate people?

[40:52] Gifts!

[42:02] Outro

[42:59] Theme song

item.description:
The story of Eisav teaches us to look beyond externalities.

🏆 Win a $1M apartment in Yerushalayim! - https://TheDreamRaffle.com

👉 Promo code ORLOFSKY for $10 off!

~~~

This episode is brought to you by Am Yisrael Chai’s 5th Annual Dream Raffle!

Have you ever dreamed of owning a luxury apartment in the heart of Yerushalayim? One with views of Har Habayis and a $1,000,000 value!

By entering the 5th Annual Dream Raffle, brought to you by Am Yisrael Chai, that dream can be a reality!

Enter now at https://TheDreamRaffle.com to take advantage of a special two tickets-for-one deal and be sure to use discount code ORLOFSKY at checkout for an additional $10 off!

For your chance to win, go to https://TheDreamRaffle.com. for your chance to win the Yerushalayim apartment of your dreams!

___

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

"In the Rav’s Yom Kippur story with R’ Levi Yitzchak, you mentioned how Yossele asked for a Machzor instead of Mashiach. Why was that held against him? How are we supposed to know when there is an Eis Ratzon for something?" (Via anonymous)

"Given the recent elections in Israel and the USA, I was wondering the following: Why does the Torah require us to have a king? History has shown all the problems that a total monarchy has. Even the Jewish kings in Tanach routinely had problems with corruption and abuse of power (for example, King Achav killing the innocent Navos and hunting down the Neviim, Queen Atalya killing almost all the dependents of David, Menashe forcing everyone to worship the Baal, other kings who killed the Sanhedrin, etc.)Is democracy really worse than having a complete tyrant on the throne? Why does the Torah require a king when the position is (and indeed was) so easily corruptible?” (Via anonymous)

"It’s said that words have power, so we must be careful what we say. On the pre-YK podcast you said, to make a point, ""I cut off my leg with a power saw."" Why is it OK to make this and other bold statements, that others would not?" (Via Shoshana Schwartz)

"Suppose you have a friend that wants to arrange a Shidduch between your child and his, and you know it's a bad idea. Is there a way to politely turn down the Shidduch and still remain friends? Thank you" (Via anonymous)

"Can you speak about being "frummer than Hashem" - doing Chumras that in essence don't help you grow in your Ruchniyus?" (Via anonymous)

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

[37:53] Q&A: Jewish Kings vs democracy?

[47:40] Q&A: Making bold statements?

[52:00] Q&A: Politely declining a shidduch?

[53:57] Q&A: Extra Chumras?

[55:59] Outro

[56:31] Theme song

item.description:
Rabbi Orlofsky reflects on 4 years of the show, and on how to make great plans at the very start.

🏆 Sponsored by https://TheDreamRaffle.com - win a $1M apartment in Yerushalayim!

This episode is brought to you by Am Yisrael Chai’s 5th Annual Dream Raffle!

Have you ever dreamed of owning a luxury apartment in the heart of Yerushalayim? One with views of Har Habayis and a $1,000,000 value!

By entering the 5th Annual Dream Raffle, brought to you by Am Yisrael Chai, that dream can be a reality!

Enter now at https://TheDreamRaffle.com to take advantage of a special two tickets-for-one deal and be sure to use discount code ORLOFSKY at checkout for an additional $10 off!

For your chance to win, go to https://TheDreamRaffle.com. for your chance to win the Yerushalayim apartment of your dreams!

___

❓ Questions of the Week

"What is the hardest topic/time of year to prepare a speech or drasha for?" (Via Yaakov Jaroslawicz)

"You often talk about the idea that Hashem understands our nature and what we are and aren’t capable of. But how does that fit with the various Chazals that describe severe punishments for certain aveiros? (ein lo chelek bolam haba, kareis, children dying, etc.)?Are they just to scare us?" (Via "Benny Malek")

"When is someone halachically obligated to forgive someone? If Person A calls Person B to ask for forgiveness many times, is Person B doing an aveirah if she doesn't answer or says she can't forgive because she's not there yet?" (Via BK)

"Why did Hashem create us with wisdom teeth if many times they don't grow in straight and many get them pulled?" (Via anonymous)

"If it is against the Torah to endanger your life, why did Chana & her 7 sons do so?" (Via Dina)

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Why is it so hard to find happiness during Sukkos and Simchas Torah?

_

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a zechus for the donors

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

"I just heard the car wash story from a few weeks ago. Were the car wash guys correct in not giving in and holding up the whole line?" (Via Lazer Cohen)

"Why does the Torah allow us to eat grasshoppers? Why would we want to eat bugs?" (Via "Malphigian Tubule Expert")

"What is the meaning of leather shoes in Judaism? We can't wear them on Yom Kippur or Tisha B'av. We can't wear them in the Mishkan or Beis Hamikdash. They must be worn during chalitzah. Also, are we specifically supposed to wear leather shoes during the rest of the year? What is the meaning of all this? (Via anonymous)

"It seems that there is a trend in frum publication and advertising that women’s faces can never be shown. Many women, including myself find this to be demeaning, upsetting, and absurd. What are your opinions about this trend? (Via Nechama)

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

item.title: The Miracle of Teshuva (Ep. 185)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Teshuva is the greatest favor of all -  we can undo the past!

_

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a zechus for the donors

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

"How did reshaim like Hitler yemach shemo survive a single Rosh Hashana and Yom Kippur? Surely they were written in the book of death? (Via Shimon Guttman)

"What is the nicest thing someone ever said/did for you? (Via anonymous)

"If the sun is Din (loved this show by the way), why it able to heal Yaakov Avinu after his struggle with Eisav's malach?" (Via Risyl Edelman)

"No women were involved in the Eigel or Meraglim. Why didn’t that make a difference to the outcome? Following that line of reasoning, can we women have an impact on the future without the men?We always hear about the nashim tsidkoniyos, but in this case that didn’t seem to help the outcome…" (Via Risyl Edelman)

"Why is it that every Gemara which is Torah Sheba'al Peh, have to be printed in the same exact layout, but Chumash which is Torah Shebichsav, could be printed in different layouts. Shouldn't it be the opposite?" (Via anonymous)

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

item.title: Big Dreams (Ep. 184)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Hashem can give you anything this year. What will you ask for?

_

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a zechus for the donors

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

"Are the other planets the previous worlds that Hashem created and then destroyed?" (Via anonymous)

"Why do you think modern art became so popular? It seems to me that it's random splashes of paint" (Via anonymous)

"Do animals have neshamos? The Derech Hashem says no, yet the Kav Hayashar and many chassidishe stories speak of animals being gilgulim. I'm trying to understand if the Ramchal's view is incompatible with chassidishe thinking, in which case, what's the Nekudas hamachlokes? Or can the 2 views be reconciled? Being that you have given Messilas Yesharim shiurim for many years i was hoping you could provide perspective on the Ramchal." (Via Yehuda Kravetz)

"I love the show! Question - I've always been uncomfortable with the mitzvah of killing the 7 nations and Amalek, including women and children. I think most of us would be horrified at the idea of going into an Amaleki village and actually killing everyone - parents holding their children, pregnant women, going into the nursery and killing all the babies, nurses and staff, grandparents, schoolteachers, artists, musicians, doctors, special needs kids, etc. it's absolutely horrifying and sounds exactly like the Nazis, Chas veShalom! What's your approach to a mitzvah that at face value stands for unspeakable viciousness and brutality?" (Via anonymous)

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

item.title: I'm Not Perfect (and that's OK)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Teshuva begins when we admit that we have what to work on.

_

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a zechus for the donors

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

"Do personality tests like Dr. Jung’s measure the actual middos that a person is born with? Do these tests correspond at all with Jewish hashkafa?" (Via Banish Benjamin)

"I was wondering if the Rav can explain the concept of Kiddush Hachodesh with messengers, especially on Rosh Hashana. Did that mean that everyone was working as if it were a regular day, and then Shluchim come and suddenly it became Yom Tov and everyone has to blow Shofer?" (Via anonymous)

"Upon entering a new Zman, can you give some insight on how to avoid burnout, especially when starting with so much enthusiasm?" (Via anonymous)

"If an adult is doing something you think isn't so safe (ex. young kids playing in the front yard without supervision, ignoring doctors strong recommendations for a young child's/baby's nutrition) Is it ever your place to get involved as a parent/in-law/sibling/friend? If so, any suggestions how to do so in a way that will be received?" (Via Chana G)

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

~~~

item.title: Worthy of Judgement (Ep. 182)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Elul teaches is that we're capable of taking responsibility for our lives.

_

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a zechus for the donors

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

"Why did Chazal only give a list of Kosher Birds and not signs like other animals. Didn't chazal know that this would lead to an unclear mesorah about which birds we can and cannot eat? (Via Benni Malek)

"Why would Hashem bring dementia to old people and have them lose their memories? They seem like they’re just not with us here. Who suffers more - us or them? Thank you"

"You've mentioned in the past that your parents referred to you as a "lo yutzlach". Did they do the same to your siblings? If not, why only you?" (Via "Rabbi Orlofsky Crazy Number One")

"I learned how to drive last year. BH I happen to be a good and responsible driver. When I first started driving, I would be really nervous and daven extra hard that morning that when I drove, I would drive safely and not hurt anyone else. (I’m a generally anxious person, if you can’t tell.) Now, when I drive, I don’t feel so nervous anymore. I daven but I don’t worry about it as much during tefillah. And not worrying makes me worry… is Hashem going to see my lack of worry as me being too confident in my skills (eg kochi votzem yudi) instead of believing it’s Hashem who keeps me safe no matter how experienced I am?" (Via "YK")

~~~

Chapters

[00:00] Intro

[00:40] Dedication

[1:06] Topic: Worthy of Judgement

[32:57] Q&A: Signs of kosher birds?

[35:24] Q&A: Why dementia?

[40:49] Q&A:  Why are you called a "lo yutzlach"?

[45:01] Q&A: Overconfident?

[51:22] Outro

[51:47] Theme song

item.title: What a Wonderful World (Ep. 181)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Summer is the time to revel in the beauty of God's creation.

_

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a zechus for the donors

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

"I know this is a weird question: If Hashem isn't bound by time, why can't we daven for the past if Hashem is there now? (Via anonymous)

"What bracha is shnitzel? I recently heard that it might be mezonos, but I always thought chicken was shehakol. Can you please explain? (Via anonymous)

"If the Rav can meet anyone in the world, who would it be and what would you say to them? (Via anonymous)

"Why is it that whenever there is some sort of crisis for example 9/11, COVID, Russia invading Ukraine etc. someone finds a Chazal or Sefer that says these exact details mean Mashiach is coming. Don't we believe Achakeh Lo B'chol Yom Sheyavo? why is everyone getting so excited about a war? (Via anonymous)

"You mentioned some people who were 'traumatized' from the story of the Netziv. I think I may be (read: for sure am) one of those people. The attitude that I can always be living life better than right now can be tormenting and robs me of my yishuv hada'as after every big decision. 'maybe I could've been more, maybe I should've chosen that path, maybe when I get to shamayim everything I DID do will pale next to what I could have done. Maybe if I was a better person I would have been zoche to a more holy job/family/friends/hadracha and I'll be chayav for all the lost opportunities’, etc.

The thoughts are not constructive and yet I can't let go of them – because aren't they true? How can we be honest about the fact that we are imperfect and have much room for growth, and still have an attitude that inspires growth and not anxiety and despair?

I remember hearing my father quote the Rosh Yeshiva, Rabbi Leibowitz zt""l say 'If it's bringing you closer to HaShem it's the yetzer tov, if it's bringing you further away, it's the yetzer hara' - but it's hard to fight such a religious yetzer hara to me! This is a really big struggle for me. Any practical tips would be appreciated."

(Via Chana G)

~~~

Chapters

[00:00] Intro

[00:55] Dedication

[1:33] Topic: What a Wonderful World

[30:20] Q&A: Praying for the past?

[30:52] Bracha on a schnitzel?

[34:07] If you could meet anyone?

[40:27] Crisis and Moshiach?

[42:08] Traumatized by Netziv story?

[55:09] Outro

[55:58] Theme song

item.title: The Good Things We Do (Ep. 180)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Let's find some great activities to do with our families this summer.

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a zechus for the donors

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

Any suggestions on ways to affect change and inspire a community of people to spend less? (via anonymous)

How come it is general practice to substitute Hashem's Name when singing Shabbos zemiros, eg. "Kah Ribon" instead of Y-ah Ribon"? It seems like this was not the original intent based on how they are written. Thanks. (via anonymous)

Some people daven for years and Hashem ignores them. Some people get everything without davening. What does that mean? Does davening work or not? (via anonymous)

Where does alcohol play a role in Judaism? We know that we make Kiddush and Havdalah every week on wine, as well as get married over wine. It is a chosheve drink that is at every simcha and many Shabbos and Yom tov meals. Yet on the flip side, we see how dangerous the effects of alcoholism can be. Any insight will be appreciated (via anonymous)

~~~

Chapters

[00:00] Intro

[00:16] Sponsorship

[00:37] The Good Things We Do

[35:03] Q&A: Hashem's name in Zemiros?

[37:08] Can we make people spend less?

[40:21] Does davening work?

[42:33] Alcohol and Judaism

[44:51] Outro

[45:28] Theme song

~~~

item.title: Tisha Bav: Speak Softly (Ep. 179)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
In today's world, nothing pays off like being thoughtful with our words.

🏆 Sponsored by Eli and Shira in honor of our anniversary. Thank you Rabbi Orlofsky for keeping us inspired and entertained! Thank you to our parents for helping us so much with our kids and for everything that else that you do for us!. May we continue to bring you nachas and simcha for the next 100 plus years!

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

"Do you have any suggestions for being Dan L'kaf Zchus?" (Via anonymous)

"How are we supposed to balance feeling happy, blessed and seeing the good in every thing, and feeling sad that we are in Galus, realizing how much we are missing and yearning for Geula?" (Via anonymous)

"If someone texts Lashon Hara to you privately, or on a group chat, what's the appropriate response? Is there a way to show disapproval without being preachy? Is it permissible to sympathize with the intent of helping them see a different perspective?" (Via Chana G)

~~~

Chapters

[00:00] Trying something different...

[4:28] Dedication

[4:54] Tisha B'av & Marriage

[8:38] Tisha B'av: Speak Softly

[36:06] Q&A: How to be Dan L'kaf Zchus?

[39:36] Balancing gratitude with sadness in Galus?

[42:20] How to respond to Lashon Hara via texting?

[45:40] Closing

~~~

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

0. "How does someone stay calm and level-headed while they are frustrated in an agitating situation?" (Via Jay Gelb)

0. "What do I do if I impulsively get angry? How do I stop?"

0. "In most cases how come you find that kids especially teenagers get along with grandparents better then parents?" (Via anonymous)

0. "The Rav has a reputation for being funny. Is it ever hard to be funny? Does this ever cause a pressure to be funny when you're not interested?"  (Via anonymous)

0. "How was it for the Rav to move to Eretz Yisroel in regard to 2 day Yom Tov? Thank you for such a wonderful show!"  (Via anonymous)

0. "Is it possible to learn the entire Torah?"  (Via anonymous)

item.title: Constant Kindness (Ep. 175)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Rabbi Orlofsky reflects on the amazing people and experiences he encountered on his recent trip to the USA.

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

"Can you explain the Chazal that Torah can be a "sam hachaim" (potion of life) or a "sam hamaves" (a poison). How can learkgn Torah have a negative effect on someone?

"How do you gain self-confidence? How could I not let other people effect me? For example you once spoke how you let people make the same joke about the matzah tie every year and you didn't care? Thank you for making the podcast and especially the question answer segment- I really enjoy it!"

"You've mentioned in the past how Adam and Chavah symbolize the ideal marriage - one man, one woman. If so, why are men halachically allowed to have multiple wives? And once you've answered that, by the same logic why can't a woman have several husbands?

"I have a question about evolution - of the Rabbi Orlofsky bookshelf. Is it a real bookshelf in Rabbi Orlofsky's house? When did the shelf become a green screen? Does Rabbi Orlofsky travel with a backdrop of the bookshelf? If Rabbi Orlofsky got a new bookshelf, would the green screen change as well?

~~~

Chapters

[00:00] Intro

[00:32] Not a fun day

[2:35] Constant Kindness: USA trip

[32:14] Q&A: Torah as a poison?

[34:42] How to gain self confidence?

[41:58] Polygamy in halacha?

[47:09] Bookshelf Q&A?

[51:20] Outro

[52:38] Theme song

item.title: Live in Flatbush 2, with Rabbi YY Rubinstein (Ep. 174)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Rabbi Orlofsky's old friend and former BBC broadcaster Rabbi YY Rubinstein joins the show to talk humor, sensitive audiences, and the BBC's antisemitism on this extended episode, recorded in front of a live audience in Flatbush.

Learn more about Rabbi YY's latest initiative at https://ner-tamid.com

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

🎉 Check out the Meaningful Tefila series, on Zoom: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

Event produced by Ari Rosner

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

"What is your approach to bringing secular references in front of a frum audience?

"Is it permitted for a Jewish person to read the Harry Potter books? If no, why not? (Submitted via audience member)

"What is the purpose of non-Jews suffering? I can understand Jews wanting to get a tikun (gilgul, teshuva, etc) but what about non-Jews?

"Can we please hear Rabbi YY's American accent?

"How does on get Rabbi Orlofsky Show merch?

"I try to be a good example to my family, siblings, parents, and I don't notice significant changes in their lives. How much should I try to get them more involved with Jewish values?

"Is today's generation expected to adapt to technology? And if yes, what is the right amount?

"What is the Rav's view of Bachurim on an off-shabbos davening at a 9 AM minyan? Is it OK if they catch up and get Sof Zman tefila?

"Could you please do the magic trick from episode #13 with the tie, just slower?

"You used to be the only Rabbi YY, now there's two of them! (Via Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky)

~~~

Chapters

[00:00] Intro

[3:21] Introducing Rabbi YY

[4:41] Tales from the BBC

[34:07] Q&A

[34:52] Secular references

[51:44] Harry Potter books

[59:47] Non-Jew suffering?

[1:07:00] Rabbi YY's American accent?

[1:13:40] ???

[1:14:23] How to change others?

[1:17:40] Adapting to technology

[1:22:48] Bochurim davening late

[1:26:40] Magic trick from Ep. 13

[1:27:40] Two Rabbi YY s?

[1:31:23] Outro

[1:31:30] Theme song

~~~

item.title: Dairy Delight (Ep. 173)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
What's the deeper meaning of having dairy on Shavous?

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

🎉 Just started: The Tefila series! http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

"Is there a real future for Jews in America? I feel that we live in a time when there is not only a spiritual decline but a spiritual decay - something that is rotten deep inside. Everything might look nice on the outside but there is clearly no passion and real devotion to Yiddishkiet. Can that change or is there no real future here?" (Via anonymous)

"When a person leaves this world, it's nice if they can leave behind something that can inspire the next generation. This could be books, or in your case podcasts and recordings which will continue to influence people. What else can a person leave behind in this world that will help future people? I would also like to leave something down in this world for even one person to be inspired by." I don't think writing a book is for me (who knows?) but do you have any other ideas? (Via anonymous)

Why did Hashem make teenagers so reckless? What use is there in that? (And if you want to go there - why do they physically mature at the same time? It seems like a recipe for disaster, and often is) (Via anonymous)

What do you do when you're bored? (Via anonymous)

~~~

Chapters

[00:00] Intro

[00:17] Shavous: Dairy Delight

[24:26] Q&A: Jewish American Future?

[27:02] Q&A: What to leave for future generations?

[28:52] Q&A: Reckless teenagers?

[32:54] Q&A: What do you do when you're bored?

[35:01] Outro

[35:34] Theme song

~~~

item.title: When Life gets Messy (Ep. 171)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
No matter how big the mess, we can clean up and start over.

🎉 Just started: The Tefila series! http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila

🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor

___

❓ Questions of the Week

📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

"With all the discussion about R' Chaim Kanievsky's hasmadah (finishing Shas every year, etc), isn't it discouraging? I'm just a normal guy and will probably not finish Shas even once it my lifetime! How can we normal people hear such gedolim stories and not be discouraged? (Submitted by anonymous)

Why can't women be halachic witnesses? (Submitted by anonymous)

Could you please say the story about how your brother burnt down the kitchen? (Submitted by anonymous)

~~~

Chapters

[00:00] Intro

[00:29] Live in Flatbush 2!

[2:00] 🎉 Tefila series is live!

[3:26] Topic: When Life gets Messy

❓ Questions of the Week

"Would you consider starting a shidduch aspect to your website to let young single entering the parsha use your shuirim as a starting reference point? As listening to your podcast already means there must be a lot in common! (Submitted by anonymous)

"As someone who has ADD, what do you do when (if) you get overwhelmed? Thank you for so much chizuk! (Submitted by anonymous)

"Over my life, there have been times that I've seen things that I shouldn't have. As much as I try not to think about these inappropriate thoughts, they sometimes come back anyway. Is there a way to not think bad thoughts and to keep a clean mind? (Submitted by anonymous)

"I heard Rabbi Orlofsky mention coming up with an approach to korbanos (in an old shiur) - can you please discuss at some point, maybe before Sefer Vayikra? (Submitted by Adina R)

❓ Questions of the Week


📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa


"Why are conservative and reform (and also less frum) people so into the idea of tikkun olam? Where does it come from? (Submitted by Leah, age 16)

""Why did we take the Christian numbering system of the Bible and use it to split up the Torah into Parshios, Perakim, and Pesukim? Since when do we take things from the non-Jews and apply it to our Torah? (Submitted by R Cohen)

"What do you think about large tzedaka organizations that have no transparency or public financial accountability for the money? Should one donate to such organizations, or is it a waste? What tzedaka do you recommend donating to? (Submitted by anonymous)

item.title: Pesach: Returning to Life (Ep. 168)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Why did Yetzias Mitzrayim happen specifically in the springtime?



🏆 This episode is sponsored by anonymously. May the z'chus of today's learning be a bracha for a smooth and complete refuah shelayma for Miriam Chaya bas Chanitah...with love from Ima and Aunt Bayla. Thank you Rabbi Orlofsky for enriching our lives each and every week!


🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor 


___



🏆 Sign up for the upcoming Tefila series: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila


___



❓ Questions of the Week


📝 Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa


"When the Rav was reminiscing about your trip to America, you spoke how you're telling us all of this because you miss us and you want to connect. However, the Rav is talking to a camera. How can you feel such a connection to something that you don't actually see, And to people you don't actually know? (Submitted by anonymous)

"Is there a connection between Ha Lachma Anya, and Chad Gadya, since they are the only things in the Haggada that are in Aramaic? (Submitted by anonymous)

"An egg is pareve. A chicken is fleishig. At what point does the change occur? (Submitted by anonymous)

"Why do Chazal say in Sanhedrin 97a that Moshiach will only come with Hesech HaDa'as, when we give up waiting for him, when we Daven every day in Shemoneh Esrei that we await the Yeshua, and in Ani Maamin it says we wait for Moshiach to come any day? Thank you for doing this show, it is a highlight in our family's life! (Submitted by Yehuda Winzelberg)

"Is it true Rabbi Orlofsky doesn't know his wife's favorite color? What is the best gift you ever got your wife? (Submitted by S Skier)

"If the only reason why we laugh is because of the unexpected, then why do we sometimes laugh at our own jokes? (Submitted by anonymous)


~~~


Chapters


[00:00] Intro

[00:16] A special thank you

[5:57] Dedication  https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor 

[7:25] Pesach: Returning to Life

[27:50] Q&A: Aramaic in the Hagadah?

[32:54] Q&A: Eggs are parve/chicken is fleishig?

[36:47] Q&A: Moshiach coming b'hesech hadaas?

[42:18] Q&A: R' Orlofsky's wife's favorite color?

[45:06] Q&A: Laughing at out own jokes? 

[46:21] Outro

[47:11] Theme song



~~~

item.title: The Age of Legends (Ep. 166)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
R' Chaim Kanievsky's passing marks the end of an age of Torah heroes.

🏆 This episode is sponsored by Eli & Rena Gray in honor of our son Abie's first (Hebrew) birthday. Thank you for continuing to inspire your audience each week with your unique blend of humor and passion for truth. We are grateful to have both had you as our rebbe and hope that Abie will be able to meet you soon and continue the legacy. Hatzlacha!


🤗 Sponsorship opportunities: https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor 


~~~


▶️ R' Orlofsky's talks in the USA:


Project Inspire 2022: Sunday Keynote "It’s All About You"

https://www.torahanytime.com/#/lectures?v=180741 


Project Inspire 2022: "I'm a Atheist"

https://www.torahanytime.com/#/lectures?v=181072


Project Inspire 2022: "Suspending Our Disbelief": How to Build Emunah in Hashem

https://www.torahanytime.com/#/lectures?v=180715


A Talk in LA:

https://www.torahanytime.com/#/lectures?v=182091



___



🏆 Sign up for the upcoming Tefila series: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila


_



❓ Questions of the Week: 

"Why is it that among less affiliated Jews there are some Mitzvos that they are very makpid on e.g - fasting on Yom Kippur, Pesach and Bris Milah. These are all quite difficult mitzvos and I wonder why these are generally well observed. If I wasn't affiliated these aren't necessarily the ones I would choose? (Submitted by Geoff Dennis)

"Is there such a thing as "don't let people take advantage of you" in Judaism?" (Submitted by anonymous)

"I've noticed that many people (especially men) from a Yeshivish background have a sloppy, unkempt appearance. Spills, unclean clothes, lack of hygiene. Why is this? What happened to kavod Hatorah? Why don't they care about looking right?" (Submitted by anonymous)

"If men don't like it when their wives repeat the same request why don't they at least make any movement or noise to acknowledge that they heard and understand the first time?" (Submitted by Confused Wife)



Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa


rabbiorlofsky.com/book-events to book R' Orlofsky for live speaking engagements!


~~~


Chapters

[00:37] Dedication

[01:46] USA trip recap

[14:00] Catching up

[20:26] The Age of Legends

[32:11] Q&A section

[32:24] Why are difficult mitzvos more popular?

[36:22] Being "taken advantage of" in Judaism 

[43:31] Frum people appearing sloppy?

[36:22Being "taken advantage of" in Judaism ] Married men not acknowledging their wives?

[53:37] Outro

[54:18] Theme song



~~~

❓ Questions of the Week:

"Who created Hashem - doesn't everything have a beginning point?" (Submitted by the audience)

"What is the Halacha to drink on Purim in Aramaic?" (Submitted by the audience)

item.title: Purim: Expect the Unexpected (Ep. 164)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Purim can help us recognize how little we really know.


___



🏆 Sign up for the upcoming Tefila series: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila


_



❓ Questions of the Week: 

"How come whenever I drink tea I get a pain in my eye?"

"What should a person drink on purim?

"The Gemara says that Vashti didn't want to come before Achashverosh because she had grown a tail. That's right at the beginning of the Megillah. Why is that significant?

"What is the punishment for forgetting מוריד הטל ?

"During Purim we hear about the Amelekites. We hear about the Hittites, the Edomites, and the Caananites. What it the significance of the Samsonites?

"What is the opposite of progress?

"What is faster, to New York or by bus?



Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa


rabbiorlofsky.com/book-events to book R' Orlofsky for live speaking engagements!


~~~

item.title: Mishkan, the Universe, & Everything (Ep. 162)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Why does the Torah hide important everyday halachos in rare historical occurrences like the Mishkan?


🏆 Sign up for the upcoming Tefila series: http://rabbiorlofsky.com/tefila


_


🇺🇸 Rabbi Orlofsky is still on tour in the USA!


Here's where to see him:


March 4-5  - Los Angeles, CA (Anshei Emes)


_



❓ Questions of the Week: 

"Would Rabbi Orlofsky prefer if people wrote their real names when submitting questions?" (Submitted by anonymous)

"How do I know when I'm ready to start shidduchim?" (Submitted by anonymous)

"If you have a full time job, are in a paying kollel and your wife works and you don't overspend, is taking a loan to survive justified?" (Submitted by anonymous)

item.title: Tefila: Women's Gift, & Listener Feedback (Meaningful Tefila #10 - Ep. 157)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
R' Orlofsky reacts to the massive response to the Meaningful Tefila series, and reflects on women's unique connection to Tefila.

_


🏆 "Sponsored by Yitzchak & Miryam Gindi. Most motsai shabbatot you’ll find us sitting in front of the computer watching the Rabbi Orlofsky podcast learning & laughing. We appreciate your knowledge and insight and enjoy your humor and wit. Thank you for inspiring us, May you be blessed to continue doing great things for Am Yisrael in good health!"


_



❓ Questions of the Week: 

"How do you stay motivated?" (Submitted by anonymous)

"What can we do between washing and Hamotzie?" (Submitted by anonymous)

"What would you say to the many people who are disappointed that corona didn't end with the coming of Moshiach? (Submitted by anonymous)

"One, what are your thoughts regarding the opinion that African Americans are from חם and should therefore be slaves? Two, do you think it’s ok to have young kids coming home with some extremely racist things? (as a student I have heard firsthand)" (Submitted by anonymous)



Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa


rabbiorlofsky.com/book-events to book R' Orlofsky for live speaking engagements!

item.title: Waterbury Returns (Feat. Waterbury Mesivta) (Ep. 156)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
The boys from Waterbury return to sing and discuss the challenges that face young creative frum men, self-expression, and making truly meaningful music, on this special Shabbos Shira musical episode!


_


🏆 Sponsored by Chicago Chesed Fund in honor of their 5th annual Shas for Shidduchim Campaign. https://Shas4Shidduchim.org


This Tu B'Shvat, on Monday, January 17th, hundreds of lomdim will complete all of Shas in 24 hours, as a  z’chus for Klal Yisroel’s singles. Sponsor a daf on behalf of yourself or up to 5 other singles at https://Shas4Shidduchim.org


_


Guests


Singers:

Moyo Orlofsky

Dovid Edell

Shlomo Sonenberg

Eli Dachs


Guitar by Moyo Orlofsky

Bass & additional instruments by Michoel Samuels


Song list:

[3:13] Song: Uvinei

[33:22] Song: Kirvas Elokim

[58:12] Song: Thank You Mommy

[1:01:37] Song: V'Ahavascha (Please Don't Take Away the Love)


~~~


Chapters


[0:00] Intro 

[3:13] Song: Uvinei

[6:05] New mug!

[6:51] Shas4Shidduchim.org

[12:04] Introductions

[18:03] Topic: Artistic outlets for frum men

[33:22] Song: Kirvas Elokim

[36:20] Topic: Jewish music, secular music, and parodies

[58:12] Song: Thank You Mommy

[1:01:37] Song: V'Ahavascha (Please Don't Take Away the Love)

[1:04:04] Q&A: Favorite instrument?

[1:06:45] Q&A: If Steven Spielberg was frum?

[1:14:22] Outro 



-


❓ Questions of the Week: 

"What is your favorite instrument? (submitted by anonymous)

If Steven Spielberg was a completely Orthodox Jew (not working on Shabbat, doing only kosher work) he wouldn’t be able to create movies like Jaws or Indiana Jones. Isn’t that denying the world entertainment and denying him his creativity? (submitted by Jonathan M)



Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

item.title: Praying Together (Meaningful Tefila #9 - Ep. 155)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Our prayers are best in special places - with special people (conclusion of the Meaningful Tefila series.)

_


🏆 Sponsored by Chicago Chesed Fund in honor of their 5th annual Shas for Shidduchim Campaign. https://Shas4Shidduchim.org


This Tu B'Shvat, on Monday, January 17th, hundreds of lomdim will complete all of Shas in 24 hours, as a  z’chus for Klal Yisroel’s singles. Sponsor a daf on behalf of yourself or up to 5 other singles at https://Shas4Shidduchim.org



❓ Questions of the Week: 

"How do we determine the balance between when Raban Gamliel said that he can't just put on Shabbos begadim to greet his father- as Eisav did- because he wasn't on that level, and he didn't want to look like a faker; and the concept of "Mitoch shelo lishmah, ba lishmah"? (submitted by anonymous)

"Wondering if there is anything that can be proven absolutely? Do I just have to believe, or can something be proven? (for example - my existence)" (submitted by anonymous)

"How can there be a machlokes on a metzius? Let's say did this and this happen to the yidden in the midbar, etc." (submitted by Daniel Levy)


Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa


rabbiorlofsky.com/book-events to book R' Orlofsky for live speaking engagements!



~~~

item.title: How to Talk to God (Meaningful Tefila #8 - Ep. 154)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
When you feel Someone is really listening, your tefila will never be the same.


🏆 Sponsorship: "Dear Rabbi Orlofsky, I greatly enjoy listening to your podcast and even sometimes go back to listen to older episodes. This episode is sponsored anonymously as a zechus for a zivug hagun for the donor and for another single relative." (https://rabbiorlofsky.com/sponsor )



❓ Questions of the Week: 

"Can you please explain the whole concept around Sotah? It can’t really be about a couple having an affair and only the woman gets punished, can it?"

Is there a substantial difference in the Ashkenazi good hechsherim in Israel, or is it all politics?(submitted by Tova Black)


Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

item.title: Get Ready to Change your Davening (Meaningful Tefila #6 - Ep. 152)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Take your Tefilos to the next level by preparing in advance!


🏆 Sponsorship: "Dear Rabbi Orlofsky, I greatly enjoy listening to your podcast and even sometimes go back to listen to older episodes. This episode is sponsored anonymously as a zechus for three couples I know should each have a healthy & happy child this year b'shaah torah."



❓ Questions of the Week: 

"What do think about giving your kids things that you perceive as luxuries or extras, if everyone else in the class actually has it? For example, for weekly pizza day in school, every child in my daughter's kindergarten class has been ordering pizza- do I get it for my kid so she won't feel resentful being the only one left out, or will she learn resilience (and my value of saving special things for saving special occasions)?" (Submitted by anonymous)


Submit your questions at https://rabbiorlofsky.com/qa

item.title: Just Love Them (Ep. 139)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
The key to Kiruv is having a deep appreciation for people.


🏆 לעילוי נשמת ראובן אביגדור בן חיים מרדכי who was niftar on 8 Sivan, just two months shy of his 100th birthday. My father-in-law's first activity every morning was to sit at his desk and write numerous checks to assorted Tzedakot. He was very proud of his son - my husband - for his accomplishments for the Jewish People. תהא נשמתו צרורה בצרור החיים


(Dedication appears at the end of the episode)


~~~

🏆 Sponsorship: "It's been 10 years since seminary and while I've lost touch I never stopped listening and re-listening to your shiurim from our Mesilas Yesharim classes. It was all I had for a while but 1 year ago I discovered your podcast and down the rabbit hole I went- starting with episode 1. I'm so happy that you are doing this and am honored to sponsor an episode- may you live till 120, if only to keep my spiritual wellness going- kidding. Sponsored l'eilu lishmas my brother Moshe Yoel ben Yaakov Yosef and in the zechus that my sister, Sheindel Liftcha Bas Gershana find her zivug."


Topics:

item.title: The Weirdest Shabbos (Pesach 2021 #1) (Ep. 129)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Rabbi Orlofsky walks through what to do - and what not to do - when Erev Pesach falls out on Shabbos.


🏆 Sponsored anonymously in honor of Paul Laster's a"h 5th yahrtzeit (Avraham Pesach ben Mordechai), which falls out on the 13th of Nissan



Halachos of Erev Pesach on Shabbos

Not eating Matza



Bubby Orlofsky's Chopped Liver

It's Easy, Stupid!


Take 1 lb (0.5 KG) kashered livers

Fill a medium mixing bowl with chopped onions.

Fry the onions.

Combine livers and onions in a meat grinder

Run three hand-boiled eggs through the meat grinder to clean out to any remaining liver


(If you don't have a meat grinder, use a food processor)


Serve on a lettuce leaf with three cucumber rounds, two red pepper slices, a green pepper circle, and a radish cut in the shape of a flower.




~~~

item.title: Rabbi, Should I Marry Him? (Shidduch Series #10) (Ep. 124)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
R' Orlofsky explores the path from proposal, to engagement, to planning, and finally the Chuppah. (Extended 63m episode)


🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a zechus for the Mashiach!

item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Age? Finances? Handicaps? Culture? Divorces? Language gap? Social stigmas? Rabbi Orlofsky breaks down these common shidduch considerations.


🏆 Sponsored anonymously leilui Nishmat Yisrael ben Yaakov. I hope my contribution will help others to continue to be inspired by you.


Topics covered:

Age

Height

Finances

Professions

Handicaps, illnesses, and genetics

Culture

Chassidish/Litvish

Language

Divorced Families

Family Problems

Baal Teshuva/Ger

item.description:
Before discussing marriage, we need to explore the roles of men and women  in Judaism. 


🚸 May not be suitable for younger audiences


🏆  Sponsoring for Nov 7 (#116), in memory of Kalman Moshe ben Reuven Avigdor (Kalman Packouz) on the 1st yahrzeit (18 Cheshvan). Through his original, trail-blazing Torah Faxes, Emails, First-Ever Jewish Computer Dating Service (back in the '80s), Western Wall WebCam, overall influence and unconditional love for the Jewish People, this world became a much better place because of him, but he left a huge hole in the hearts of all who knew and loved him.

item.title: 113: Off to a Great Start (2nd Anniversary!)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
As we start the year afresh, it's time to discuss the power - and challenge - of new beginnings.


🏆 A sponsored as a zchus for our children, on the occasion of the bar mitzvah of our son, Moshe Gavriel, on Shabbos Parshas Bereishis.



Rabbi Orlofsky's Updated Onion Soup

It's Easy, Stupid!™



• Slice 3 large onions into rings. Fry in oil until they darken to adeep

• Sprinkle a little flour unto the onions. Mix until it forms a thick place

• Add kosher salt, pepper, curry

• Add 2 liters (8 cups) water, bring to simmer

• Add 3-4 diced red onions

• Add another 2 liters water, bring to a boil

• Swirl in some dry white wine

• Check and clean 1 leek. Slice into sounds and add to soup.

• Add another 2 liters water. Boil until soup thickens

• Garnish with shredded cheese and croutons


~~~


Sign up for R' Orlofsky's live online shiurim!

item.title: 111: But It's Only Derabanan!
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
The year's Yomim Tovim can teach us a lot about our true priorities.


🏆 Sponsored by Shmuel Arnold in honor of the marriage of my daughter Naama Leia who has always been a student of yours. When she was young, I used what I learned from your shiurim on answering questions to answer her questions. When she was old enough, I gave her access to your shiurim so she could learn how to answer questions herself, and she has been a source of chizuk wherever she goes. She used your mehalach when she spent a summer at Brandeis and was confronted by an anti-religious professor. She used it to answer questions in high school, and in seminary. We owe you a tremendous debt of gratitude for all you have done for us, and we thank you for both your words of encouragement and your sense of humor. Most importantly, we thank you for your honesty, and permission to be imperfect in a world that has very little room for mistakes. Keep up the good work!


~~~

item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Never lie about potential problems. They will always come back to bite you.


Prelude: When is it OK to be critical of Eretz Yisrael?


🏆 Sponsored in memory of Chaya Sara bas R' Tzvi. She was a holocaust survivor who came to Eretz Yisroel after the war and raised a Torah family with mesirus nefesh. Today she has close to 50 descendants who are all bnei torah and Yorei Shomayim.

item.title: 89: One More Chance
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Pesach Sheni teaches us to reclaim lost opportunities.

🏆 Sponsorship opportunities available

The Orlofsky Family Matza Brei recipe

It's Easy, Stupid!

2.5 matzah, broken into small pieces

Pout hot water on top, shake in strainer

Place in mixing-bowl, add 4 eggs

Beat with a little salt

Add oil to a large frying pan

Pour in batter

Cover with large Corelle plate

Fry until slightly liquidy, flip fry other side

~~~

Moyo Orlofsky (Vocals, Guitar)

item.title: 63: I Used To Wear Pink Shirts
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
It's far too easy to judge people by the way they do - or don't - dress.


🏆 Sponsored l'ilui nishmas R' Moshe Aaron ben R' Yerucham Fishel

🌎 Learn more about sponsorships: bit.ly/orlofsky-sponsor


More pictures of the dress code of pre-war European Yeshivas:

• US Holocaust Museum Website http://bit.ly/34oe8sr

• Mir Yeshiva Photos by U. Oregon http://bit.ly/2WzKPR9



Rabbi Orlofsky's Onion Soup

It's Easy, Stupid!™


[Starts at 4:47]


• Slice 3 large onions into rings. Fry in oil until they darken

• Sprinkle a little flour unto the onions. Mix until it forms a thick place

• Add 2 liters (8 cups) water, bring to a boil

• Add 3-4 chopped red onions

• Add another 2 liters water, bring to a boil

• Swirl in some dry white wine

• Add salt, pepper, curry to taste

• Add another 2 liters water. Boil until soup thickens

• Garnish with shredded cheese and croutons



~~~

item.title: 61: A Heavenly Embrace
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Sukkos teaches us about Hashem's love for his people.

🎂 Sponsored in honor of the bar mitzvah of Yaakov Shmuel Shenker. Mazel Tov!

Lyrics to א סוכה'לע א קליינע

[Starts at 10:09]

א סוכה'לע א קליינע,

פון ברעטעלעך געמיינע,

האב איך מיר א סוכה'לע געמאכט.

באדעקט דעם דאך,

מיט א ביסעלע סכך,

זיץ איך מיר דארט סוכות ביינאכט.

באדעקט דעם דאך,

מיט א ביסעלע סכך,

זיץ איך מיר דארט סוכות ביינאכט.

- - -

דער ווינט א קאלטן,

וואס בלאזט דורך די שפאלטן,

עס לעשן זיך די ליכטעלעך פיל.

עס איז מיר א חידוש,

ווען איך מאך מיר דארט קידוש,

און די לעכטעלעך זיי ברענען גאנץ שטיל.

עס איז מיר א חידוש,

ווען איך מאך מיר דארט קידוש,

און די לעכטעלעך זיי ברענען גאנץ שטיל.

ביים ערשטן געריכט,

מיט א בלאסן געזיכט,

טראגט מיר מיין טאכטעריל אריין.

זי שטעלט עס אוועק,

און זאגט מיט גרויס שרעק,

טאטע'ניו די סוכה פאלט באלד איין.

זי שטעלט עס אוועק,

און זאגט מיט גרויס שרעק,

טאטע'ניו די סוכה פאלט באלד איין.

- - -

זיי נישט קיין נער,

האב נישט קיין צער,

זאל דיר די סוכה נישט טוהן באנק.

עס איז שוין גאר,

באלד צוויי טויזנט יאר,

און די סוכה'לע זי שטייט נאך גאנץ לאנג.

עס איז שוין גאר,

באלד צוויי טויזנט יאר,

און די סוכה'לע זי שטייט נאך גאנץ לאנג.

item.title: 54: Simanim Explained
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
The details and meaning of the Rosh Hashana Simanim.


Rabbi Orlofsky's Tzimmes

It's Easy, Stupid™!

1. Peel & slice 2 packages of carrots

2. Place in pan with little oil

3. Add flanken (spare ribs) and

4. When halfway done, pour out excess gravy

5. Coat meat & carrots with honey

6. When meat is almost done, cover and bake until carrots are soft

Serve warm

item.title: Pesach Program 2: Plan Smart
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Pesach Program 2: Plan Smart
Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky
Download

Cut the stress by planning your Pesach in advance.

🏡The Rabbi Orlofsky’s Pesach Program™!  New episodes every 2 days!

Pan-Fried Hamburgers

It’s Easy, Stupid!™ 

  • Fry chopped onions

  • Form chopped meat into patties (add garlic)

  • Add hamburgers to onions

  • Fry until cooked through

Rabbi Orlofsky’s Charoses

It’s Easy, Stupid!™ 

Combine:

  • Grated apples (make sure to taste a few)

  • Hand-chopped walnuts

  • Wine

  • Cinnamon

item.title: 23: “Not a Show, it’s a Shiur” (with special guest Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
23: “Not a Show, it’s a Shiur” (with special guest Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky)
Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky
Download

In our first special Purim edition, Rabbi Orlofsky takes a moment to discuss everything except Purim.

Bubby Orlofsky’s Matzah Brei

It’s Easy, Stupid!™ 

(If you do not eat gebruchts, stop here)

  • Crumble 2 and a half machine matzos 

  • Place in strainer. Pour hot water to soften. Drain.

Mix with:

  • 4 eggs

  • Salt


Bake covered (this makes it easy to flip)

Bake until golden brown.

Flip when halfway baked

~~~

item.title: 19: The Big Lie of Tznius
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
19: The Big Lie of Tznius
Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky
Download

Let’s recapture our dignity by talking about what tznius actually is.

🏆 Sponsored anonymously as a זכות for the donors.

~

Bubby Orlofsky’s Chinese Rice

It’s easy, stupid™! 

  • Prepare 2 cups rice

  • In a pan, brown 1 pound ground beef

  • Pour off extra grease

  • Wait for rice to cool

  • Scramble 8 eggs

  • Mix into rice

Add:

  • 5 chopped scallions

  • soy sauce 

~~~

It’s easy, stupid™

  • Add 1 pkg flanken

  • Add 3-4 cans Hunt’s tomato sauce

  • Add water & sugar

Boil for 3 hours

item.title: 15: Rabbi Orlofsky, the Musical (feat. Rabbi Binny Fogelgarn)
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
15: Rabbi Orlofsky, the Musical (feat. Rabbi Binny Fogelgarn)
Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky
Download

Rabbi Orlofsky hosts a band in honor of Shabbos Shirah.

"Shabbos Angels" (single)

(Released in support of The Shabbos Project 2014)

Composed by: Binny and Tunni Fogelgarn

Rabbi Binny Fogelgarn - Guitar, Vocals

Binyomin Goldschmeidt - Guitar, Vocals

Yaakov Saffer - Vocals

Avraham Yee - Vocals

Supporting crew -

Leib Meltzer

Dovi Cohen

Chaim Rosenbaum

Bass and additional instruments by Michoel Samuels

Original Music Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OCIOXR9sqJw

Download the free single from: https://mostlymusic.com/products/binny-tinny-shabbos-angles

For more information, please contact rabbifogelgarn [at] gmail [dot] com

Lyrics 

Yes it is a big deal

Every  Friday night is a big meal

There's always lots of guests

They're all dressed in their best

Zemiros sung with zest

And everyone is blessed

By the shabbos shabbos angels...

By the By the shabbos shabbos angels

Dishwashing 

Ovens on

Music playing 

Nothings wrong

Clean it well

Challah smell

Oh I love preparing for my...


Shabbos clocks

Shabbos socks

Silverware 

White tablecloths

Keys for locks

And tissue box

Oh I love preparing for my shabbos

Oh I love preparing for my...

Chorus

Showers hot

Mikvahs not

Chicken soup 

On blech in pot

Kiddush cups 

Lchaim shnapps

Oh I love preparing for my...

Flowers green

House is clean

Table set

How serene

Mishpacha my magazine

Oh I love preparing for my shabbos

Oh I love preparing for my...


Chorus

Kugel rice

Cake smells nice

Crockpot Cholent 

Taste a bite 

Shabbos lamp

Gotta tape the lights

Oh I love preparing for my...

Shabbos clothes

Hats and bows 

Stop! There goes 

Your weekday woes

STOP!

Oh I love preparing for my shabbos

Oh I love preparing for my...

Chorus

Good Shabbos now...

~~~~

item.title: Episode 13: Not a Shiur
item.dc:creator: Michoel Samuels
item.description:
Episode 13: Not a Shiur
Rabbi Dovid Orlofsky
Download

The show vs. shiur debate is finally settled, and insights into the psychology of Pharoah’s magicians.

~~~~~

FREE Recipe:

Bubby Orlofsky’s Fleishig Veggie Soup

In an 8 quart pot, add:

  • 1 pkg Flanken (chulent meat)

  • 3 onions, diced

  • 3 zucchini

  • 6 carrots

Fill with water. 

Boil for 2 hours.

Then add:

  • Small package frozen mixed vegetables.

  • ½ cup barley

  • ½ cup beans

  • Salt & Pepper (to taste)


Cook until beans are soft.

~~~~~
